codeReview.summary: Compared against the prior failed review, this fix commit directly addresses the remaining null-coercion gap by adding null-to-empty-string coercion for source.standard, source.section, metadata.extracted_by, and metadata.qc_status, and adds targeted tests for null and non-null behavior. The original blocking issue from fix-scrutiny-post-processor-bugs is resolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure adherence reporting for python-worker appears inconsistent: the skill requires test-first TDD, but this run's transcript skeleton shows implementation before adding tests while handoff.skillFeedback reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md:29 requires "Write tests first (TDD)"; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 9ded7c4b-3827-48e8-92c3-6f934506cefb includes "Now let me implement the changes" before "Now let me add the test cases"; handoff file .../handoffs/2026-03-28T23-19-03-921Z__fix-null-coercion-top-level-fields__9ded7c4b-3827-48e8-92c3-6f934506cefb.json has skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: PASS: The fix adequately resolves the prior blocking null-coercion failure by covering the previously missing required top-level string fields and adding corresponding tests; no blocking code issues found.
